arts.sum: Simon Hooper's decision not to send off Ibrahima Konate for his challenge on Matheus Cunha in Liverpool's win over Wolves was heavily criticised by Richard Keys

arts.ttl: Preview: League leaders visit Villa Park
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Liverpool - Wed, Feb 19th 2025, 19:30 Aston Villa look to return to winning ways when hosting Liverpool in midweek Premier League action at Villa Park on Wednesday evening (ko 7.30pm). Unai Emery's side will be hoping to respond from a 1-1 draw with Ipswich Town over the weekend, in the second of three-straight outings on home soil in the Premier League ahead of this weekend's visit of Chelsea. Villa have still only fallen to just one home league defeat so far this season, that loss coming in the opening home game of the campaign, with seven wins and five draws from their last 12 home outings in all competitions. In order to maintain this streak, however, they will have to keep out Arne Slot's league-leading Reds, who are still yet to lose on the road in the Premier League this season. Sitting pretty seven points clear at the top of the table with 13 games to go, Liverpool have dropped just two points from their last five top-flight outings, and enter the clash on a run of 21 league games without defeat. Stats Aston Villa’s Marcus Rashford scored five goals in eight home Premier League appearances against Liverpool for Manchester United – his most home goals against any side in the competition. Indeed, excluding penalties, the only players with more home Premier League goals against Liverpool are Sergio Agüero (7), Thierry Henry (6), and Jamie Vardy (6). With 14 goals and nine assists in Liverpool’s 13 away games in the Premier League this season, Mo Salah has the most ever goal involvements by a player away from home in a single campaign in the competition (23). He is also one assist away from becoming only the third player to assist 10+ goals in away games in a Premier League season, after Muzzy Izzet in 2003/04 (10) and Cesc Fàbregas in 2014/15 (11). Only against Brighton (7) and Arsenal (6) has Ollie Watkins scored more goals in the Premier League than he has against Liverpool (5). However, after scoring five goals in his first five league appearances against the Reds, the striker has since failed to score in his last four against them. Aston Villa’s Unai Emery has won just one of his 12 meetings with Liverpool in all competitions (D4 L7), winning the 2016 UEFA Europa League final 3-1 with Sevilla. Among opponents he has faced 5+ times in his managerial career, that win rate of 8% against Liverpool is his lowest. Liverpool are yet to lose a Premier League away game under Arne Slot, with his 13 games (W9 D4) the outright longest unbeaten start on the road by any manager in the competition’s history, surpassing the record set by John Gregory with Aston Villa of 12 games from March to November 1998. Liverpool remain the only side yet to lose away from home in the Premier League this season in 13 matches (W9 D4). In English top-flight history, on only five occasions has a side avoided defeat in their first 14+ matches on the road from the start of a campaign: Leeds United in 1973/74 (15), Liverpool in 1987/88 (15), Arsenal in 2001/02 (19), Arsenal in 2003/04 (19), and Manchester United in 2020/21 (19). Aston Villa have lost six of their last seven Premier League games against sides starting the day top of the table; the exception, however, was their last such home game, beating Arsenal 1-0 in December 2023. They have never won back-to-back home meetings with league leaders in the competition before. The last seven Premier League meetings between Aston Villa and Liverpool at Villa Park have produced a total of 33 goals, an average of 4.7 per game. Liverpool have scored at least twice in all seven of these games (20 goals in total). This exact fixture finished 3-3 last season – Liverpool haven’t failed to win consecutive away league games against Aston Villa since December 2004. Aston Villa have won just one of their last 14 Premier League games against Liverpool (D2 L11), a 7-2 victory at Villa Park in October 2020. The Last Meeting The last meeting between these two sides saw the Villans beaten 2-0 at Anfield at the beginning of November. Despite holding their own for large spells of the first half, and having penalty appeals turned down, Unai Emery's side ultimately succumbed to a goal in each half at the home of the league leaders. Darwin Núñez struck 20 minutes in after a swift counter attack from a Villa corner to hand the hosts the lead, before Mo Salah notched six minutes from time to finish a similar attack - with the visitors throwing bodies forward in the closing stages in search of an equaliser - ensuring Emery's men would travel home from Merseyside empty handed. Team News Unai Emery revealed Leon Bailey, Matty Cash and Ezri Konsa would face late fitness tests ahead of the clash, though Boubacar Kamara would miss out. The French midfielder was withdrawn just 16 minutes into Saturday's draw with Ipswich, and joins Pau Torres, Ross Barkley and Amadou Onana on the sidelines. Arne Slot does not expect Cody Gakpo to be fit for the encounter, the Dutch forward having missed Liverpool's win over Wolves at the weekend. Joe Gomez will also miss out, with youngster Tyler Morton the Reds' only other absentee. Managers' Pre-Match Thoughts "We are excited, how we will face them tomorrow. Now, Liverpool, they are the best team in Premier League, they are the best team in Europe and of course being very difficult. Hopefully we can share an exciting moment with our supporters in Villa Park." - Unai Emery "I think Villa away, when you get the fixture list, you all know that Villa away is one of the tougher ones you can face. Very good manager, always has a very good game plan. They brought in quite a lot of good players in the winter break as well and they already had a very good team." - Arne Slot

Fixtures Crystal Palace U21 Vs. Aston Villa U21 - Mon, Feb 17th 2025, 19:00 Aston Villa fell to defeat in Premier League 2 on Monday evening, losing 3-0 in South London at the hands of Crystal Palace. Two first-half goals from the hosts gave Josep Gombau’s Young Lions work to do in the second period at Sutton United’s VBS Community Stadium, as Ademola Ola-Adebomi’s deft finish gave Palace an early lead, before Dylan Reid doubled their advantage on the stroke of half-time. Despite seeing more of the ball after the break, the Villans proved unable to breach an astute Palace rearguard, as Ola-Adebomi’s second-half penalty completed the scoring to send Villa’s under-21s home from the capital empty handed. Palace were in control throughout the opening stages, beginning the match in charge, with Villa struggling to gain a real foothold. The first warning sign for Gombau’s charges came seven minutes in, as skipper Seán Grehan dragged an effort narrowly wide. But it did not take long thereafter for the Eagles to break the deadlock. Caleb Kporha did well to work space on the right against Kane Taylor, and sent a ball into the box which was neatly backheeled home by Ola-Adebomi. The hosts continued to threaten, with Rio Cardines calling Lander Emery into action, but the Villans demonstrated their threat in transition as the half drew to a close. First, Thierry Katsukunya headed Jamaldeen Jimoh-Aloba’s cross narrowly wide, before Charlie Pavey picked up the ball in space inside the area, but a vital intervention from recovering defender Jake Grante prevented him from getting a shot away. And the Young Lions would be made to rue these openings, as the hosts doubled their lead on the stroke of half-time, when midfielder Dylan Reid rolled an effort into the bottom corner from the edge of the area to send the Villans in at half-time two goals down. The Eagles retained their control in the second period and, while Villa saw more of the ball in their attempts to find a way back into the game, the visitors found goalscoring opportunities hard to come by. Lively wing-back Kporha provided a constant threat on Palace’s right-hand side, and saw a pair of decent efforts well saved by Emery just before the hour mark. But Villa could not keep out their hosts for much longer, as they found a third goal from the penalty spot to seal the points with just over 15 minutes remaining, Ola-Adebomi converting from 12 yards for his second of the evening, after a Palace forward had hit the deck inside the area. Palace successfully saw out the closing stages through the four added minutes in the bitter London cold, to record a shut-out and condemn Gombau’s side to defeat. Upcoming Fixtures Brighton and Hove Albion U21 Vs. Aston Villa U21 - Fri, Mar 3rd 2025, 19:00 Aston Villa U21 Vs. Everton U21 - Fri, Apr 4th 2025, 18:00 Stoke City U21 Vs. Aston Villa U21 - Fri, Apr 4th 2025, 18:00

arts.ttl: Matchday Guide: Aston Villa v Liverpool
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Liverpool - Wed, Feb 19th 2025, 19:30 Aston Villa are back in action on Wednesday night as Premier League leaders Liverpool visit Villa Park. Unai Emery's side face the Reds on the back of Saturday's draw against Ipswich Town in B6, looking to extend their 12-match unbeaten league sequence on home soil stretching back to August. Here's everything you need to know ahead of the encounter to enjoy your visit on matchday... Getting to the game Click on the sections below to learn more about tickets, Villa Park and transport to the stadium. Mobile Tickets Villa Park is now fully NFC ticketing enabled for supporters to access the stadium. Near-Field Communication (NFC) enables fans to enter the stadium via Apple or Google Wallet. Supporters can now also access their match-by-match tickets and enter the stadium directly via the Aston Villa app. Simply log into your Digital Account in the app and your tickets will appear under the ‘Tickets’ section. Supporters must update their app to the latest version to ensure in-app ticketing works without issue. Tickets A limited number of general admission tickets for the match remain. Book online here. The best way to secure tickets for future matches at Villa Park is via a Membership. Get Matchday Ready Ahead of the match at Villa Park, the club would like to advise supporters of the following ticketing information to ensure everyone enjoys their visit to our stadium. Fans will need to provide one of the two specified ticket types to gain entry to the stadium - access tickets in our official Aston Villa mobile app or download to Apple/Google Wallet for NFC entry. Hospitality and Premium Experiences The club has a range of hospitality and GA+ areas to complement our existing offer. Check out all the information and book online here. Hospitality bookings for our upcoming home matches can be made over the phone on 0333 323 1982 (option 1) or by emailing hospitality@avfc.co.uk. Under-3s Policy Many supporters may not be aware of the policy regarding under-3s, so we are reminding supporters that under-3s are only permitted to be in the Family areas in the Trinity Road stand at Villa Park and must have a match ticket. Further information can be found here Aston Villa Ticketing (avfc.co.uk) Visiting Villa Park It's important that supporters take steps to plan their visit to the stadium. Turnstiles open at 6pm, so make sure you arrive at the stadium in plenty of time ahead of kick-off to ensure smooth entry. Please note, access to stiles is maintained during the first half and will close once the half-time whistle is blown. However you're planning on getting to the match, take a look at our Travel & Parking guide before leaving home. A traffic exclusion zone will be in operation around the stadium, meaning there will be less traffic on Trinity Road and Witton Lane from up to two hours before the match starts. The Holte Pub will open three hours prior to kick-off and close two hours after full-time. Parking and Travel Supporters are advised that the supply of car parking around Villa Park is limited. Fans can pre-book their space via either of the links below: www.astonvillaparking.co.uk/football/events www.countrywidemanagementsolutions.co.uk We will continue to work with local parking operators to offer fans the opportunity to book parking near the stadium. We would strongly encourage all supporters who have not pre-booked their car parking to travel to Villa Park by alternative modes. Covered cycle parking is available at our Inner City Academy on Witton Road. Plan ahead and check train timetables for direct services or travel earlier for less busy services. Visit Transport for West Midlands’ Journey Planner or the National Rail Enquiries real-time journey planner here. There are a number of Park and Ride sites across the region, for further details visit Park and Ride. To help you plan your journey by public transport, visit How to get to Villa Park. There will be a full northbound closure of the M6 from Junctions 7 to 10 from 11pm. Supporters heading north after the game might be impacted by the closure, with a signed diversion in place. The northbound link that connects the M5 to the M6 between Junctions 7 to 8 of the M6 will also be closed. Traffic signals have been optimised around Villa Park to help improve flow of traffic away from the ground. This should assist with journey times for our AV82 city centre shuttle bus (see below). City Centre and Tamworth Shuttle Buses Aston Villa is delighted to confirm to supporters that our AV82 city centre shuttle bus service will continue to run on matchdays this season. This follows a successful trial of the service at the back end of last season. The service will be in operation for all men’s home matches in the Premier League, UEFA Champions League and all domestic cup competitions, helping supporters travel more easily to and from Villa Park. Run by Stagecoach, the AV82 shuttle picks supporters up from outside Birmingham New Street Station on Dudley Street, running directly to Villa Park. Buses run every 15 minutes, starting three hours before kick-off for every home fixture. Services run back to Birmingham New Street at regular intervals for up to 90 minutes after the final whistle of every game, with services departing from Grosvenor Road, on the walking route towards Aston Station. Traffic signals have been optimised around Villa Park to help improve flow of traffic away from the ground. This should assist with journey times for our AV82 city centre shuttle bus. The club also has a dedicated bus service, AV1, that runs to Villa Park from Tamworth on matchday. You can purchase tickets here. More information is available here. Cycle Parking Covered and secure cycle parking is available a short walk away from Villa Park at our Inner City Academy, Brook Vale, on Witton Road. Accessibility information Supporters with accessibility requirements travelling to Villa Park by rail are advised to look out for the accessibility signage on both platforms at Witton Station. Station staff will be able to help you if you wait in the designated areas. More information is available here. For any supporters with additional mobility needs who have questions about travel to and from Villa Park on a matchday, please contact accessibility@avfc.co.uk. Pre-match activities Click on the sections below to learn more about activities taking place at Villa Park before kick-off. Enjoy the Fan Zone Villa Park's new Fan Zone is again open on Wednesday, located in the former Players' Car Park. The new space opens its doors at 4:30pm, with supporters able to enjoy a bar area and food. The Fan Zone is also in prime position to watch player arrivals from 6pm. We also have entertainment taking place on the stage opposite Trinity Reception, including live music from Big Classics. Fans can also get 10% off selected items in our Fan Zone on the North Stand car park from 5pm until closing. We want to see your experience of matchday at Villa Park! Post your photos and tag #UTV and you could be featured on the big screens at the stadium! Supporters are advised that the R44 accessible entrance will be closed for a period of time. Family Fun Zone Work is soon to begin on the transformation of the existing Academy building at Villa Park. Unfortunately, due to the planned works, the Academy building is not operational to the extent to which we can safely open the Family Fun Zone. The club are working on alternate arrangements to enable a continuation of some of the family-friendly pre-match activities. Updates will follow in the coming weeks. Food and Beverage Villa Park offers a wide range of cuisine and beverages on a matchday. Outside the stadium, several street food trucks will sit alongside a bar serving alcoholic and soft drinks from 4.30pm. Supporters are reminded that Aston Villa adopts a one-cup system at Villa Park, in our continued environmental sustainability efforts. Cashless Stadium Please note, Villa Park will be cashless to help ensure safe social distancing practices and minimise contact at all points of sale. This includes the Villa Store, with card payments widely accepted in and around the stadium. The only exception is programme kiosks outside the ground, which will still accept cash. In-stadia betting kiosks will also be in operation for this fixture and such service will be provided on a cashless basis. North Grounds Update Aston Villa Football Club has announced its plans for a bold regeneration project at Villa Park that will build on the Club’s 150-year heritage, while also creating the fan experience zone of the future. Expected to go live Christmas 2025, the new project includes the launch of The Warehouse, a new multi-use venue set to become a centrepiece of the matchday experience and a hub for year-round entertainment, along with a new ticketing office, improved plaza in the North Grounds, and expanded footprint of the club shop. Admission to The Warehouse will be free for ticketed supporters on matchdays, when the venue becomes the largest beer hall in the Premier League – a vibrant space to connect, enjoy a pint, and soak in pre-match excitement with state-of-the-art LED lights and flat-screen TVs. Beyond matchdays, The Warehouse will transform into the Midlands’ premier concert venue and event space. The 3,500-seater venue will host world-class performances, from live music and sporting events to community activities, creating a versatile space for audiences of all kinds. Designed to bring the Aston and Birmingham communities together, it will also serve as a platform for local and global talent, making an impact far beyond Villa Park. More information here. Matchday Programme The latest edition of the Villa News & Record will be available to purchase at Wednesday's match! Fans can continue their sticker album collection, while the programme is also packed with features and news from around the club. Supporters will be able to purchase copies from sellers at Villa Park on matchday, while they're also available to purchase in the club shop and online here. Villa Store Click on the sections below to learn about merchandise points around the stadium. Store Opening Hours There are a number of places where supporters can shop for Aston Villa merchandise on a matchday, including the upgraded Villa Store. Below you'll find the opening times for each shop around the ground... Villa Store: 9am-10.15pm Holte Store: 4.30pm-7.30pm and 9.15pm-10.15pm 82 Lounge: 4.30pm-7.30pm and 7.15pm-10.15pm Lower Grounds: 4.30pm-7.30pm and 7.15pm-10.15pm Trinity Store: 6pm until the end of half-time Matchday offer - 50% off training kit! Those visiting Villa Park on Wednesday will be able to take advantage of an incredible matchday offer of 50% off our range of 2024/25 grey/green training kit items! This fantastic deal is redeemable IN-STORE ONLY at the above locations, and only for Wednesday's match, so make sure you head along to a retail outlet in B6 to make the most of the opportunity. During the game Click on the sections below to learn more about events that take place during the match. See it...hear it...report it! If you witness discriminatory behaviour at a match or around a stadium, please report it to the police or a steward. Supporters at Villa Park can text REPORT to 60060 along with the location of the incident and details of what you have witnessed. The text line also includes the reporting of any safeguarding concerns. The text service will be supported by our security team and West Midlands Police. Each text costs one standard message at your network rate. The club may share your details with West Midlands Police if further information is required to assist with a criminal prosecution. All-seater Stadium We would like to remind supporters that Villa Park is an all-seater stadium. In all areas of Villa Park, we have children, elderly fans and those with medical conditions who are unable to see or watch the match if supporters stand up in front of them. Please be mindful of this at all times and thank you for your cooperation. Fan Engagement Click the sections below to learn about how you can get involved on matchday and our fan loyalty programme, Villa Rewards. Get Involved New for 2024/25, the ‘Villa Predictor’ game will give fans the opportunity to win a £500 store voucher if they correctly predict the outcome of the game. T&Cs apply. We want to know about your matchday experience at Villa Park. Tweet us using #UTV and you could feature on the big screens at the stadium! Pride Rewards The Pride Rewards Portal is back up and running. Log in here. AV150 Legacy Wall Aston Villa is excited to reveal a new AV150 Legacy Wall at Villa Park as part of our 150th anniversary celebrations. Supporters have the opportunity to become part of our iconic home, wherever you are in the world, by purchasing your very own personalised plaque at Villa Park. And phase one of the installation of the Legacy Wall is closing soon, as supporters have until 11pm on matchday to secure a plaque, available from £80, and be guaranteed to have it in place at Villa Park during our anniversary season. Our Legacy Wall is a place where you, your friends and family can show your support for the club, celebrate births, marriages, anniversaries or pay tribute to loved ones. For more information, click here.

arts.ttl: Referee removed from duty as connections to clubs including Manchester City and Aston Villa investigated
arts.sum: Fans of the Premier League often wrongly insist that the officiating in the league is much worse than elsewhere in Europe. Indeed, the drama seen in Spanish football on a relatively frequent basis is much more than happens even with media related to the English top flight. Sometimes that drama is motivated by questionable decisions The post Referee removed from duty as connections to clubs including Manchester City and Aston Villa investigated appeared first on Sport Witness.
